## Honest-note checklist

- [ ] The note is factual and describes what the project / paper *is*, not what it *claims*.
- [ ] The note is fair — describes limitations a careful reader would notice, without accusing the authors of dishonesty or incompetence.
- [ ] The note does not use marketing words (revolutionary, state-of-the-art, groundbreaking, breakthrough, cutting-edge).
- [ ] The note does not give financial advice (no "buy", "sell", "you should invest").

## Tooling checklist

- [ ] `python tools/validate_entries.py` passes locally
- [ ] `python tools/build_readme.py` was re-run and the README is committed
- [ ] One entry per PR (or a clearly-scoped batch with rationale below)
